records.ety: [F. ressembler; pref. re- re- + sembler to seem, resemble, fr. L. similare, simulare, to imitate, fr. similis like, similar. See Similar.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Resembled (-b'ld); p. pr. & vb. n. Resembling (-bl?ng).]

defs: To be like or similar to; to bear the similitude of, either in appearance or qualities; as, these brothers resemble each other.
quotes: We will resemble you in that.

defs: To liken; to compare; to represent as like.
quotes: The other . . . He did resemble to his lady bright.

defs: To cause to imitate or be like.
